entries.description: Updated docs/session-state-schema.md with the Set 033 schema delta: the +2 nested fields (checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt) under orchestrator in the JSON-shape block; a dedicated 'Check-out / check-in (Set 033)' section codifying H4 identity (engine + provider composite, model below the identity line), H3 hard-coordination (refusal must name the holder + the two release paths --force and 'Release Check-Out'), the block-null-when-status-not-in-progress invariant (OQ1), migration tolerance for pre-Set-033 in-flight files, and the OQ2 documentation-alias note. Updated the 'Mid-set, session 2 in flight' + 'Between sessions' worked examples to include the +2 fields; flipped the 'Complete' worked example to orchestrator: null per the new invariant.

Refactored ai_router/session_state.py register_session_start to compute and write checkedOutAt + lastActivityAt. Same-holder branch (engine + provider composite, H4) preserves prior checkedOutAt and bumps lastActivityAt to now; different-holder branch (fresh check-out OR force-override handoff) sets both to now. Migration tolerance: a same-holder re-attach against a pre-Set-033 block missing checkedOutAt populates the field with now (one-time fidelity loss documented in schema doc).

Refactored ai_router/start_session.py: added the H3 hard-coordination gate (EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT = 4) that fires after the existing boundary checks (in-flight, closed-session re-open, skip-ahead) and refuses different-holder writes unless --force is set. Refusal message names the holder identity (engine + provider) and both release paths (--force and 'Release Check-Out' Command Palette action) per the H3 contract. Helper _identity_label() renders the composite for both the error message and the writer-log entry.

Added --force argparse flag to start_session CLI; help text covers the override scope (H3 only, not the other boundary refusals). Force-override path calls new _log_force_override() helper that appends a single line to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log (best-effort; failure does not block the override per the H3 invariant that the state file is the source of truth, log is observability). Module-level constant ORCHESTRATOR_WRITER_LOG (monkeypatchable from tests). The writer log format: '<ISO timestamp> force-override session-set=<basename> session=<n> prior=<engine + provider> new=<engine + provider>'.

Authored ai_router/tests/test_checkout_writer.py with 7 tests covering the six spec-enumerated branches: (a) fresh-start checkedOutAt == lastActivityAt; (b) same-holder re-attach preserves checkedOutAt + bumps lastActivityAt; (b') same-holder re-attach updates model + effort in place (mutable below H4 identity); (c) different-holder refusal returns EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT + does not mutate; (d) refusal message contains both holder identity AND both release paths --force + 'Release Check-Out'; (e) --force writes through + appends one entry to writer log (monkeypatched path); (f) tolerated read of in-flight set with no checkedOutAt populates the field on next same-holder write. Hit a Python 3.11 backslash-in-f-string syntax error during first run (fixed by hoisting basename().rstrip() to a local variable); all 7 tests pass. Full ai_router suite: 640 passed, 1 skipped (no regressions). Verified session_lifecycle.py is orthogonal to the H4 equality predicate (cancel/restore swap status tokens, never touch the orchestrator block).

Authored verify_session1.py following the Set 032 Session 2 template (RouteResult JSON-dump-first per feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling). Bundled the four primary deliverables (start_session.py, register_session_start function from session_state.py, test_checkout_writer.py, the Check-out / check-in section of session-state-schema.md) plus two ground-truth references (proposal-addendum §9 six locked verdicts + spec.md Session 1 only). Bundle 56,053 chars — under the verifier-spiral risk threshold per feedback_split_large_verification_bundles. Round A verdict: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues. All four relevant verdicts (H1, H3, H4, OQ1) traced to specific file:line citations; all six branches (a-f) cross-referenced to named test functions; boundary refusal ordering confirmed (1-5 existing checks, 6 H3 new, 7 writer call); edge cases verified (null provider equality, unwritable log non-blocking, no prior state file, --force on same-holder preserves checkedOutAt). Round B not required.

Audited all consumers of MarkerWatchService.resolveActiveSet() and readers/writers of .dabbler/orchestrator.json across src/, scripts/, media/, and tests. Source-side touchpoints identified: providers/MarkerWatchService.ts (the service itself), providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts (consumer), providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts (pure renderer), commands/{setOrchestratorManual,installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode,openOrchestratorWriterLog}.ts (S3 scope — keep compiling, refactor in S3), codex/configWatcher.ts (S3 scope — same), scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js (delete), tests, media/session-sets-tree/client.js. Resolved two spec-flagged in-session decisions via AskUserQuestion: (1) rename MarkerWatchService.ts → inProgressSetsService.ts (operator preference for clarity post-migration); (2) delete docs/orchestrator-marker-schema.md outright (spec default). git mv preserved blame on the rename. Renamed module exports listInProgressSets(all?) + extractRecommendation() + recommendationFor(set); the class + watcher + polling-backstop + slug-integrity check were retired entirely since the workspace-level file-watcher in extension.ts already watches session-state.json.

Refactored providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts: dropped the MarkerWatchService constructor parameter; reshaped postSnapshot to compute every in-progress row's accordion via accordionStateFromOrchestratorBlock(); buildBuckets now sorts the in-progress bucket via listInProgressSets() (startedAt asc, per spec) instead of SessionSetsModel.sortBucket's lastTouched-desc default; buildRow renders accordionHtml for EVERY in-progress row (not just the resolver's single 'active set'). Suppression key field renamed across the wire: markerUpdatedAt → accordionUpdatedAt, sourced from orchestrator.lastActivityAt (S1's bump-on-re-attach field — preserves the naturally-aging suppression semantic). Extended types.ts OrchestratorInfo with the S1 fields (provider, checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt) and updated fileSystem.ts's state-file reader to parse them. Added accordionStateFromOrchestratorBlock(block, recommendation) to providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts: synthesizes the unchanged RenderState/OrchestratorMarker shape with signalKind='current' + confidence='high' (writer is authoritative; retired last-observed/configured-default/manual signal kinds no longer apply); tier via classifyRecommendationTier(); thinking=false; updatedAt falls back through lastActivityAt → checkedOutAt → now. Returns kind='empty' on null block or missing provider+model identity.

Removed the ambiguityBanner end-to-end: types/sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts dropped SnapshotPayload.ambiguityBanner + RowPayload.isResolvedSet (renamed ToggleRowMsg.markerUpdatedAt → accordionUpdatedAt); media/session-sets-tree/client.js dropped the .ambiguity-banner DIV render path; renderRow's isExpandable predicate simplified from (isResolvedSet && accordionHtml !== null) to just (accordionHtml !== null) — multi-in-progress is the supported case; isSuppressedForRow now reads row.accordionUpdatedAt and compares against the host-echoed suppression record (the lookup is concrete now that the wire carries per-row updatedAt). DOM attribute renamed: data-marker-updated-at → data-accordion-updated-at.

git rm tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js (613-line writer). Spec risk R1 acknowledged: between S2 ship and S3 ship the Claude SessionStart hook + Codex configWatcher + setOrchestratorManual command all reference the deleted script via HELPER_REL = path.join('scripts', 'write-orchestrator-marker.js') as a string path. Compile-time clean (no symbolic imports). Runtime invocations will fail until S3 refactors those three call sites to invoke `python -m ai_router.start_session` per H1 (hooks become invokers). extension.ts updated to drop the `MarkerWatchService` import + construction; the workspace-level file-watcher already in extension.ts (watching **/session-state.json) covers every signal the new reader needs.

Deleted docs/session-sets/029-orchestrator-model-effort-gauges/.dabbler/ directory entirely (it held the one stale orchestrator.json marker file in this repo; the directory's self-protecting .gitignore meant the marker was never tracked, so only the working-tree directory needed removal). Searched for additional .dabbler/orchestrator.json files under docs/session-sets/ — none remained. Consumer-repo cleanup is S6's cross-repo CLAUDE.md notification job, not S2.

git rm docs/orchestrator-marker-schema.md per the operator's spec-default decision (delete outright vs redirect). The doc described the retired .dabbler/orchestrator.json schema; no consumers reference it for live behavior. docs/session-state-schema.md is the surviving canonical reference, already updated by Session 1 with the Check-out / check-in section.

git mv markerWatchService.test.ts → inProgressSetsService.test.ts; updated the import to point at the renamed module. Preserved all 7 extractRecommendation tests verbatim (regex contract unchanged). Authored 3 new listInProgressSets tests covering (i) filter+sort by startedAt asc, (ii) empty-result handling, (iii) tolerance of null startedAt (verifies the spec's 'oldest-in-flight first' intent treats missing timestamps as earliest). All 10 tests pass via `npm run test:unit` (Mocha stub harness). Full suite: 401 pass + 2 pre-existing failures (configEditor-foundation + notificationsSection — unrelated, neither touches changed files). `npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` clean across the full src tree.

Rewrote test/playwright/electronLaunch.ts: replaced seedOrchestratorMarker (wrote .dabbler/orchestrator.json with the retired marker shape) with seedOrchestratorBlock (merges the Set 033 Session 1 orchestrator block into session-state.json). Rewrote test/playwright/session-sets-tree.spec.ts: removed the 'configured-default Codex marker' + 'manual Gemini marker' scenarios (the retired signalKind affordance they tested no longer applies in the writer-as-authority model); added (1) 'seeded orchestrator block renders provider sublabel' + (2) 'two in-progress sets each render their own accordion body' — the second scenario asserts both rows carry data-expandable='1' AND the ambiguity banner has count 0 (the H2 + banner-removal contract painted on screen). Layer-3 scenarios not run end-to-end in this session (Set 027's note: Playwright matrix is exercised in CI on Linux via xvfb-run; local execution deferred to operator review pending S4's dedicated scenario expansion).

Authored verify_session2.py following the Session 1 template (RouteResult JSON-dump-first per feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling). Bundled the seven primary deliverables (inProgressSetsService.ts, OrchestratorAccordion.ts full file, CustomSessionSetsView.ts full file, sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts, client.js, inProgressSetsService.test.ts, session-sets-tree.spec.ts) plus the extension.ts activation excerpt (proving the MarkerWatchService import + construction were removed) plus three ground-truth references (proposal-addendum §9 H2 verdict, spec.md Session 2 only, session-state-schema.md Check-out / check-in section). Bundle 96,477 chars — larger than Session 1's 56,053 but well under the gemini-pro limits demonstrated by Session 1's clean round. Round A verdict: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues. All seven assertion groups (A H2 retirement, B synthesizer correctness, C suppression continuity, D banner removal, E Layer-2 coverage, F Layer-3 coverage, G edge cases) cross-referenced to named functions / cited code. Round B not required.
